title_en: Hadiths 1–10: Foundations of Islam, Intention, and Lawful and Unlawful
title_ar: الأحاديث 1-10: أسس الإسلام والنية والحلال والحرام
content_en:
The first ten hadiths of al-Arba'een al-Nawawiyyah establish the foundational principles upon which the entire structure of Islamic practice and ethics rests. Commentators have noted that these ten hadiths alone could serve as a comprehensive introduction to Islamic teaching.

Hadith 1 — the Hadith of Jibreel — defines Islam, Iman, and Ihsan. Islam is submission through the five pillars; Iman is belief in Allah, His angels, His books, His messengers, the Last Day, and divine decree (qadar), both its good and its harm; Ihsan is to worship Allah as though you see Him, and if you cannot see Him, He sees you. Ibn Rajab's commentary on this hadith spans dozens of pages, exploring the theological depths of each element of faith and the relationship between the three levels.

Hadith 2 — 'Islam is built upon five' — lists the five pillars in the famous sequence, providing the structural skeleton of the religion. Hadith 3 — 'Actions are by their intentions' — is perhaps the single most important statement in Islamic ethics, establishing that all deeds are judged by their inner purpose. Ibn Daqiq al-Eid notes that some scholars considered this hadith to be one-third or one-quarter of the entire religion, so many rulings depend on the question of intention.

Hadith 4 on the creation of the human being and divine decree, Hadith 5 on innovation in religion ('every newly invented matter is a bid'ah, and every bid'ah is astray'), and Hadith 6 on the lawful and the unlawful being clear with a gray area between them — 'Whoever falls into the doubtful matters falls into the forbidden, like the shepherd who grazes his flock near a sanctuary' — are three of the most widely applied legal and ethical principles in Islamic scholarship. The commentary on Hadith 6 explores the concept of ihtiyat (precaution) and the boundaries of the permissible with reference to numerous fiqh applications.
content_id:
Sepuluh hadits pertama al-Arba'een al-Nawawiyyah menetapkan prinsip-prinsip dasar yang menjadi landasan seluruh struktur praktik dan etika Islam. Para komentator mencatat bahwa kesepuluh hadits ini saja sudah dapat berfungsi sebagai pengantar yang komprehensif bagi ajaran Islam.

Hadits 1 — Hadits Jibril — mendefinisikan Islam, Iman, dan Ihsan. Islam adalah kepasrahan melalui lima rukun; Iman adalah keyakinan kepada Allah, para malaikat-Nya, kitab-kitab-Nya, para rasul-Nya, Hari Akhir, dan takdir ilahi (qadar), baik yang baik maupun yang buruk; Ihsan adalah menyembah Allah seolah-olah engkau melihat-Nya, dan jika engkau tidak dapat melihat-Nya, maka Dia melihatmu. Komentar Ibnu Rajab terhadap hadits ini mencakup puluhan halaman, mengulas kedalaman teologis setiap unsur iman serta hubungan antara ketiga tingkatan tersebut.

Hadits 2 — 'Islam dibangun di atas lima perkara' — menyebutkan lima rukun Islam dalam urutan yang masyhur, memberikan kerangka struktural agama ini. Hadits 3 — 'Amal perbuatan bergantung pada niatnya' — barangkali merupakan pernyataan terpenting dalam etika Islam, yang menegaskan bahwa seluruh amal dinilai berdasarkan tujuan batin pelakunya. Ibnu Daqiq al-Id mencatat bahwa sebagian ulama menganggap hadits ini sebagai sepertiga atau seperempat dari keseluruhan agama, mengingat begitu banyak hukum yang bergantung pada persoalan niat.

Hadits 4 tentang penciptaan manusia dan takdir ilahi, Hadits 5 tentang bid'ah dalam agama ('setiap perkara yang diada-adakan adalah bid'ah, dan setiap bid'ah adalah sesat'), serta Hadits 6 tentang yang halal dan yang haram yang sudah jelas dengan adanya wilayah abu-abu di antaranya — 'Barang siapa jatuh ke dalam perkara-perkara syubhat, ia jatuh ke dalam yang diharamkan, seperti seorang penggembala yang menggembalakan ternaknya di dekat kawasan terlarang' — merupakan tiga prinsip hukum dan etika yang paling luas diterapkan dalam kajian Islam. Komentar terhadap Hadits 6 mengulas konsep ihtiyat (kehati-hatian) dan batas-batas perkara yang dibolehkan dengan merujuk pada berbagai penerapan dalam fiqh.
title_id: Hadits 1–10: Dasar-Dasar Islam, Niat, serta Halal dan Haram
content_ar:
The first ten hadiths of al-Arba'een al-Nawawiyyah establish the foundational principles upon which the entire structure of Islamic practice and ethics rests. Commentators have noted that these ten hadiths alone could serve as a comprehensive introduction to Islamic teaching.

Hadith 1 — the Hadith of Jibreel — defines Islam, Iman, and Ihsan. Islam is submission through the five pillars; Iman is belief in الله, His angels, His books, His messengers, the Last Day, and divine decree (qadar), both its good and its harm; Ihsan is to worship الله as though you see Him, and if you cannot see Him, He sees you. Ibn Rajab's commentary on this hadith spans dozens of pages, exploring the theological depths of each element of faith and the relationship between the three levels.

Hadith 2 — 'Islam is built upon five' — lists the five pillars in the famous sequence, providing the structural skeleton of the religion. The commentary on Hadith 6 explores the concept of ihtiyat (precaution) and the boundaries of the permissible with reference to numerous fiqh applications.
